content.text: IDP Service Catalog for Claude Components

An Internal Developer Portal (IDP) service catalog is the authoritative registry of Claude-related components available inside the organisation. Without a catalog, engineers discover Claude components through word of mouth, Slack searches, or inspecting other teams' codebases — each discovery path is slow, error-prone, and produces inconsistent results. With a catalog, the approved prompt templates, shared client libraries, and application scaffolds are discoverable in seconds, with ownership, version, documentation, and current health status visible in one place.

attrs.alt: Diagram: IDP service catalog taxonomy — Prompt Templates, Client Libraries, and App Scaffolds as the three top-level categories with example catalog entries

content.text: What Belongs in the Claude Service Catalog
The Claude service catalog has three distinct component types, each with different metadata requirements and lifecycle rules:

versioned, approved system prompts that have passed the full CI gate pipeline. Each entry links to the YAML file in the prompts/ registry, the current scorecard status, the owning team, and the eval suite. Catalog consumers use a template by referencing its template_id and version — they do not copy-paste the prompt.

the shared Anthropic API client packages (Python: claude-client-py, TypeScript: claude-client-ts). These are the approved way to call the API — they apply retry logic, rate-limit headers, cost telemetry, and safety baseline injection automatically. New clients should install the catalog library, not call anthropic.Anthropic() directly.

project templates for new Claude features (similar to create-react-app but for Claude integrations). A scaffold pre-wires the client library, CI gates, scorecard instrumentation, and a sample eval suite. Engineers starting a new Claude feature use the scaffold rather than building from blank files.
Every catalog entry must include a standard metadata block. The IDP reads this from a catalog-info.yaml file at the root of the component's repository or directory:

# catalog-info.yaml (Backstage-compatible)
apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: invoice-assistant-template
  title: Invoice Assistant Prompt Template
  description: |
    Customer-facing prompt template for invoice explanation and billing dispute
    escalation. Owned by the billing team. Covers DO: explain line items,
    escalate disputes. DO NOT: issue refunds, access other accounts.
  annotations:
    claude/template-id: invoice-assistant
    claude/template-version: "1.2.0"
    claude/model-id: claude-sonnet-5
    claude/safety-baseline-version: v3.1
    claude/scorecard-url: https://dashboards.internal/scorecard/invoice-assistant
    claude/eval-suite: evals/invoice-assistant/
    claude/runbook: https://wiki.internal/runbooks/invoice-assistant
  tags:
    - claude
    - prompt-template
    - customer-facing
    - billing
  links:
    - url: https://github.com/org/prompts/tree/main/customer-facing/invoice-assistant.yml
      title: Prompt YAML
    - url: https://wiki.internal/process/invoice-assistant-dod
      title: Definition of Done

spec:
  type: prompt-template
  lifecycle: production    # one of: experimental | staging | production | deprecated
  owner: group:billing-team
  system: claude-billing-integration
  dependsOn:
    - component:claude-client-py
    - resource:anthropic-api
Catalog Entry Types and Their Differences
Feature team that owns the use case
When prompt content, model, or safety baseline changes
When SDK version, retry policy, or telemetry changes
When conventions, CI templates, or dependencies change

A Claude component must be registered in the IDP catalog before it can be used in production. Unregistered components are detected by the architecture drift gate in CI. Registration is a two-step process:
Create the catalog-info.yaml file in the component's directory with all required fields.
Open a PR to the catalog registration repository (or configure the IDP's auto-discovery for the prompts/ directory). The CODEOWNERS for the catalog must approve.
The IDP ingests the catalog-info.yaml on merge and the component appears in the catalog within minutes.
The architecture drift gate is updated: the component's name and version are added to the approved list in architecture.json.
A component is deprecated when a new version supersedes it. The deprecation process:

# catalog-info.yaml — deprecated template
metadata:
  name: invoice-assistant-template-v1
  annotations:
    claude/template-id: invoice-assistant
    claude/template-version: "1.0.0"    # old version
  links:
    - url: https://wiki.internal/migrations/invoice-assistant-v2
      title: Migration guide to v1.2.0

spec:
  type: prompt-template
  lifecycle: deprecated    # catalog shows deprecation warning to users
  owner: group:billing-team

Teams using a deprecated template receive a CI warning on their next PR. After 90 days, deprecated templates are removed from the catalog and the architecture drift gate blocks any code still referencing them.
What the shared client library provides
The catalog client library entry documents not just where the library is but what guarantees it provides — the contract that consumers depend on:

# catalog-info.yaml — shared Python client library
apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: claude-client-py
  title: Claude Python Client Library
  description: |
    Shared Anthropic API client for Python services. Provides:
    - Automatic retry with exponential backoff (3 retries, jitter)
    - Rate-limit header injection from the service account
    - Structured cost telemetry (input_tokens, output_tokens logged on every call)
    - Safety baseline auto-injection (current canonical version)
    - Async and sync interfaces
  annotations:
    claude/sdk-version: "0.40.0"   # pinned anthropic SDK version
    claude/breaking-change-policy: |
      Major version bumps require 4-week notice in #claude-platform.
      Breaking changes are never pushed to existing major versions.
  tags:
    - claude
    - library
    - python

spec:
  type: library
  lifecycle: stable
  owner: group:developer-experience
  dependsOn:
    - resource:anthropic-api
    - resource:rate-limit-service-account
Installation and usage from the catalog

# In a new Claude integration — discovered via IDP catalog
# pip install claude-client-py==3.1.0  (version from catalog entry)

from claude_client import ClaudeClient, ClaudeClientConfig

# Factory function — reads service account credentials from env
client = ClaudeClient.from_env()

# Or with explicit config
config = ClaudeClientConfig(
    service_account="invoice-assistant",  # maps to a rate-limit allocation
    enable_cost_telemetry=True,           # required; cannot be disabled
    enable_safety_baseline=True,          # required; cannot be disabled
)
client = ClaudeClient(config)

# All calls use the shared client; no direct anthropic.Anthropic() needed
response = await client.create_message(
    model="claude-sonnet-5",
    max_tokens=1024,
    system=template.system_prompt,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_message}],
)
How scaffolds work in the IDP

An app scaffold in the IDP catalog is a Backstage Software Template that engineers invoke through the IDP portal. The scaffold creates a new repository (or directory) pre-configured with all required Claude integration components:

# scaffold template definition (Backstage Template)
apiVersion: scaffolder.backstage.io/v1beta3
kind: Template
metadata:
  name: claude-feature-python
  title: Claude Feature (Python)
  description: |
    Creates a new Python Claude integration with:
    - claude-client-py wired up
    - Sample eval suite (10 placeholder cases)
    - CI pipeline (Gate 1–4 + scorecard)
    - architecture.json baseline
    - catalog-info.yaml pre-filled
    - Runbook template
  tags:
    - claude
    - scaffold
    - python

spec:
  owner: group:developer-experience
  type: service
  parameters:
    - title: Integration details
      required: [name, owner_team, template_id, model_id]
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
          description: Integration name (e.g. invoice-assistant)
        owner_team:
          type: string
          description: GitHub team slug for CODEOWNERS
        template_id:
          type: string
          description: Prompt template ID from the catalog
        model_id:
          type: string
          enum: [claude-sonnet-5, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001]
          description: Claude model to use (must be on approved list)

  steps:
    - id: fetch-base
      name: Fetch base template files
      action: fetch:template
      input:
        url: ./skeleton
        values:
          name: ${{ parameters.name }}
          owner_team: ${{ parameters.owner_team }}
          template_id: ${{ parameters.template_id }}
          model_id: ${{ parameters.model_id }}

    - id: publish
      name: Publish to GitHub
      action: publish:github
      input:
        repoUrl: github.com?repo=${{ parameters.name }}&owner=org

    - id: register
      name: Register in catalog
      action: catalog:register
      input:
        repoContentsUrl: ${{ steps.publish.output.repoContentsUrl }}
        catalogInfoPath: /catalog-info.yaml
Each catalog entry exposes the current scorecard status so engineers browsing the catalog can see at a glance whether a component is healthy before adopting it:
Weekly scorecard computation, pushed to catalog API
Green / Amber / Red badge on the catalog entry
Percentage shown under the component title
GitHub PR with security-reviewer approval label
Label shown prominently; deprecated entries show a warning banner

The IDP service catalog is the single source of truth for approved Claude components inside the organisation. It has three component types — prompt templates, client libraries, and application scaffolds — each with a standard metadata schema that captures ownership, version, scorecard health, and dependency information. Registration in the catalog is required before production use; unregistered components are blocked by the architecture drift gate. The catalog reduces discovery time from hours (searching Slack, reading codebases) to seconds, and ensures every engineer starting a new Claude integration begins from an approved, instrumented, health-tracked baseline.
Prompt templates in the catalog are the only approved source for system prompts — copying from a colleague's PR bypasses review, versioning, and the scorecard.
Client library entries document not just the package version but the guarantees the library provides: retry policy, cost telemetry, safety baseline injection.
App scaffolds pre-wire the full paved road: CI gates, scorecard instrumentation, catalog registration, eval suite, and runbook template.
Catalog health fields (scorecard status, eval pass rate, open blockers) allow engineers to assess a component's health before adopting it.
Deprecation is a first-class lifecycle state in the catalog: teams referencing deprecated components receive CI warnings and have 90 days to migrate before the component is removed.
